title: Method of integrated chemical and bacterial leaching of base metal
abstract: A method of integrated chemical and bacterial leaching of a base metal from a mineral is disclosed. The method includes leaching a base metal from a mineral with an ammoniacal thiosulfate chemical leaching solution having a pH from about 8.5 to about 10.5 to obtain a first leachate, subsequently adjusting the first leachate to a neutral pH in a range from about 6.0 to about 8.0, then inoculating neutralized first leachate with one or more neutrophilic bacteria, and bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated first leachate containing the neutrophilic bacteria to obtain a second leachate. The method may further include inoculating the second leachate with chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria at an acidic pH, and further bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated second leachate at the acidic pH.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of integrated chemical and bacterial leaching of a base metal from a mineral, comprising: (a) leaching a base metal from a mineral with an ammoniacal thiosulfate chemical leaching solution having a pH from about 8.5 to about 10.5, thereby forming a first leachate having a first concentration of the base metal; (b) subsequently adjusting the first leachate to a neutral pH in a range from about 6.0 to about 8.0; (c) inoculating neutralized first leachate with one or more neutrophilic bacteria; and (d) bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated first leachate containing said one or more neutrophilic bacteria, thereby forming a second leachate having a second concentration of the base metal. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the ammoniacal thiosulfate chemical leaching solution comprises one or more ammonium compounds. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the ammonium compounds comprise an ammonium halide, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium thiosulfate, or a combination thereof. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the thiosulfate is in a form of alkali metal thiosulfate, ammonium thiosulfate, or a combination thereof. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the ammoniacal thiosulfate chemical leaching solution comprises sodium thiosulfate, ammonium chloride, and ammonium hydroxide. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the sodium thiosulfate is in a concentration from about 0.05 to about 0.5 molar (M), the ammonium chloride is in a concentration from about 0.05 to about 1.0 M, and the ammonium hydroxide is in a concentration from about 0.05 to about 1.0 M. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the method further comprises adding a nitrate salt in the neutralized first leachate before said bioleaching. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein the neutrophilic bacteria comprises Thiobacillus denithficans, Starkeya novella, Paracoccus pantotrophus, sulfur oxidizing Bacilli species, or a combination thereof. 9. The method of claim 1 , wherein the chemical leaching solution is recirculated through the mineral for a first predetermined period of time, or until the first concentration of the base metal reaches a first target concentration of the base metal. 10. The method of claim 1 , wherein the inoculated first leachate is recirculated through the mineral for a second predetermined period of time, or until the second concentration of the base metal reaches a second target concentration of the base metal. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the bioleaching in (d) is carried out at a reaction temperature from about 12Â°C to about 40Â°C. 12. The method of claim 1 , wherein the base metal comprises copper, nickel, zinc or lead. 13. The method of claim 1 , wherein the mineral comprises atacamite, malachite, copper wad, copper pitch, chrysocola, covellite, chalcocite, bornite, chalcopyrite, a mineral containing nickel, a mineral containing zinc, or a combination thereof. 14. The method of claim 1 , wherein the leaching and the bioleaching are carried out in a form of heap leaching, tank leaching, vat leaching, or dump leaching. 15. The method of claim 1 further comprising: (e) inoculating the second leachate with one or more chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria, when the second leachate reaches an acidic pH of 4.5 or less 

 after said bioleaching in (d); and (f) further bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated second leachate containing said one or more chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria at the acidic pH. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria comprises Acidithiobacillus thiooxidans. 17. The method of claim 1 , wherein the method further comprises leaching a precious metal from the mineral with the chemical leaching solution in (a), and the first leachate further comprises the precious metal. 18. A method of integrated chemical and bacterial leaching of a base metal from a mineral, comprising: (a) leaching a base metal from a mineral with a chemical leaching solution comprising sodium thiosulfate, ammonium chloride, and ammonium hydroxide and having a pH from about 8.5 to about 10.5, thereby forming a first leachate having a first concentration of the base metal; (b) subsequently adjusting the first leachate to a neutral pH ranging from about 6.0 to about 8.0; (c) inoculating neutralized first leachate with one or more neutrophilic bacteria; and (d) bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated first leachate containing said one or more neutrophilic bacteria, thereby forming a second leachate having a second concentration of the base metal. 19. The method of claim 18 further comprising (e) inoculating the second leachate with one or more chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria, when the second leachate reaches an acidic pH of 4.5 or less after said bioleaching; and (f) further bioleaching the base metal from the mineral with inoculated second leachate containing said one or more chloride-tolerant acidophilic bacteria at the acidic pH. 20. The method of claim 18 further comprising adding a nitrate salt in the neutralized first leachate before said bioleaching in (d).
